verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: "Default to a monorepo"; A also answers early but with a softer qualifier: "my default recommendation is a monorepo".
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly to one recommendation while still naming exceptions; A is also decisive but spends more space framing a decision survey.
verdict.fabrication: A: "hundreds of projects" and "almost never" are broad unsupported claims; B: "thousands of devs, giant histories" is also unsupported. Both make unverifiable scale claims, but neither invents user-specific facts.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the concrete setup in one place: "one repo, three top-level service directories plus a shared package, per-service deploy pipelines".
